marketState: neutral - near-term bullish weather tilt vs. longer-term bearish storage/technical overhang
summary: Front-month NG is at $2.943, essentially at fair value ($2.917 now, spread +$0.026, status FAIR across all horizons out to +21D), so there is no valuation edge in either direction. Cooling demand is a genuine bullish standout: +15.9% vs 5y over the next 7 days and +23.9% over days 8-16, both near the top of their 5y ranges (rangePos 0.92-0.93) and rising (+22.7 in 24h). Working against that, price sits 10.2pp below its seasonal median (-13.6% YTD vs -3.4% median) with low seasonality reliability, storage is +184 Bcf above the 5y average after a bearish +72 Bcf build surprise, and every technical timeframe from 5m to 1w shows a bearish trend with a 50<200 EMA cross (though 4h RSI at 25.1 and 1d closing below its lower Bollinger band flag a stretched, bounce-prone downtrend). Managed money is net short (-60,295 contracts) but only at the 35th percentile of its 5y range, leaving room for short-covering if the heat persists. Net take: near-term fundamentals and oversold technicals favor a tactical bounce, while the medium-term picture (storage surplus, weak/low-reliability seasonality, broad technical downtrend) argues for patience before committing to a multi-week direction.

longTerm.rationale: Seasonality leans bearish (only 31% chance of a higher price in 4 weeks, typical 4-week move -3.1%) but reliability is flagged Low (50pp spread), and storage is +184 Bcf above the 5y average after a bearish +72 Bcf surprise build - both structurally bearish. That is offset by 8-16 day cooling demand running +23.9% above normal, a genuine bullish counterweight, plus COT managed-money shorts at only the 35th percentile (not stretched). With fair value itself just drifting from $2.917 to $3.068 over the next 21 days and price sitting inside every band with no edge, the conflicting signals don't net out to a clean multi-week direction yet.
longTerm.invalidation: A confirmed close below roughly $2.75 alongside another bearish storage surprise would favor fading toward the $2.42-$2.50 band low; a pullback into the $2.50-$2.60 zone while cooling demand stays elevated would favor buying toward $3.00-$3.10.
longTerm.trigger: Wait for either a breakdown below ~$2.75 with fresh bearish storage data (short setup), or a dip into the $2.50-$2.60 fair-value band while weather demand remains hot (long setup).

shortTerm.rationale: Near-term cooling demand is running +15.9% vs 5y (7d) and +23.9% (8-16d), both near the top of their ranges and still rising, while price is right at today's fair value ($2.917) with the front month slightly backwardated to Sep/Oct - consistent with the market pricing current heat. The 4h RSI at 25.1 and the 1d close below its lower Bollinger band show a stretched short-term downtrend that is ripe for a mean-reversion bounce.
shortTerm.invalidation: A sustained close below $2.78, or the 8-16 day demand outlook rolling back toward/below normal, would invalidate the long.

keyDrivers: Cooling demand +15.9% (7d) / +23.9% (8-16d) vs 5y and still rising - the strongest bullish signal
Storage +184 Bcf above 5y average after a bearish +72 Bcf build surprise
Price is fairly valued across every horizon (now through +21D) - no mispricing edge either way
All technical timeframes show a bearish trend with a 50<200 EMA cross, but 4h/1d are oversold
Managed money net short (-60,295) but only at the 35th percentile of 5y - not an extreme positioning
risks: Weather forecasts can shift quickly, reversing the near-term demand tailwind
Weekly storage surprises (build/draw vs forecast) can swing price sharply and override technical setups
